channel.title: Iran on 2 More Weeks
channel.description: Recent content in Iran on 2 More Weeks

item.title: US Claims 'Guardian Angel' Of Strait Of Hormuz, Peace Dividend Two Weeks From The Toll Booth
item.description: On Monday, President Donald Trump told Fox News’ “Fox & Friends” that the United States would “probably run” the Strait of Hormuz and “should be reimbursed” for doing so. “We’re going to keep the strait, and we’ll probably run it. We’ll become the guardian of the strait. Maybe we’ll call it the guardian angel of the strait,” he said, adding that other nations “are very wealthy” and “we can’t be expected to do that for nothing.”

item.title: Iran War Un-Terminated, And I Keep Noticing The Whole Thing Behaves Like A Stress Response
item.description: President Donald Trump formally notified Congress this weekend that the United States is once again at war with Iran, according to a letter dated July 10 and obtained by POLITICO. The letter states that the strikes that began on July 7 constitute “military action consistent with my responsibility to protect Americans and United States’ interests both at home and abroad.” U.S. Central Command has said American forces struck more than 300 Iranian military targets over three nights this week.

item.title: Iran Truce Enters Its Fourteenth Straight 'Two Weeks,' Administration Confirms
item.description:
In a statement released beneath a wall calendar that has not been flipped since April, The 2weeker Administration confirmed on Saturday what observers had long suspected: the ceasefire between the United States and Iran is, once again, good for approximately two weeks.

It has now been good for approximately two weeks for the better part of a season.

A fortnight, renewed

The arrangement began on 8 April 2026 as a tidy, fourteen-day pause, brokered by Pakistan and described at the time as a “temporary ceasefire for two weeks.” True to the spirit of the thing, it was then extended, redeclared as an indefinite ceasefire, collapsed at the Islamabad talks, was papered over with something called the Islamabad Memorandum, and — when fighting resumed on 8 July — was declared over by President Trump on 7 July before being walked back the following morning as not the sort of thing that would lead to long-term action.
